title: Lophogyne aripuanensis
taxonomy: Lophogyne aripuanensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is native to Brazil. This species is classified within the genus Lophogyne. The classification of Lophogyne aripuanensis places it in the order Poales. This classification helps to group similar species together and understand their evolutionary relationships.

intro: Lophogyne aripuanensis, also known as Lophogyne, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Lophogyne aripuanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as an infusion, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in traditional medicine to make teas and other herbal remedies.
